content: State-of-the-art active learning agent memory architectures (2024–2025) can be reengineered to dynamically weight intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM and RND methods based on how long ago an agent last encountered a given state, pruning outdated, low-information curiosity drives to avoid redundant exploration. This creates a 'curiosity forgetting' mechanism that prioritizes novel, high-impact exploration over re-exploring low-value previously seen states.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
